steps.title: Identify the problem-and-solution structure
steps.content: The passage first identifies a research problem: European colonial sources underestimate the actual power Choctaw women held in traditional roles. It then presents Pesantubbee's response: supplement those sources with Choctaw oral tradition and archaeological evidence.

steps.title: Why each choice is right or wrong
steps.content: A is wrong because Pesantubbee does not say colonial sources should be avoided — she says they should be supplemented with additional evidence. C is wrong because the text does not list advantages of a particular method, nor does Pesantubbee criticize any approach. D is wrong because the text does not suggest pursuing a different topic. B is correct because it captures the passage's two-part structure exactly: a research problem (colonial sources are inadequate) followed by a historian's approach to addressing it (using oral tradition and archaeological evidence). That matches choice B — B is correct.
